spec.title: markdown-toc
spec.summary: A standalone Markdown table-of-contents generator that walks ATX + Setext headings, handles GitHub's slug rules (incl. duplicate-heading suffixes), and round-trips with an existing TOC block (no double-insertion).
spec.rationale: Existing TOC generators (markdown-toc, doctoc) are old, npm-only, and don't match GitHub's slugify rules exactly. A modern Python implementation with property tests against the real GitHub renderer would be useful in CI for docs-heavy repos.

milestones.title: heading parser (ATX + Setext)
milestones.description: Pure function `parse_headings(text) -> list[Heading]` that returns level + raw text + line number for every ATX (`#`-prefixed) and Setext (underline-style) heading.

acceptance_criteria.description: ATX h1-h6 all parsed with correct level
Setext h1 + h2 parsed; --- under text becomes level=2
Headings inside fenced code blocks are IGNORED (hypothesis property)

milestones.technical_note: Single-pass scanner; no markdown library — we want explicit behaviour, not someone else's.

milestones.title: GitHub slugify
milestones.description: `slugify(text, seen: set[str]) -> str` that matches GitHub's rules: lowercase, spaces→`-`, drop most punctuation, keep emoji codepoints, append `-1`/`-2`/... suffix for duplicates.

acceptance_criteria.description: Property test against a curated fixture of (markdown heading -> expected GitHub anchor) pairs

milestones.technical_note: GitHub renderer is THE reference. Don't reimplement gfm-rules; encode the observed behaviour in a fixture table.

milestones.title: TOC renderer + idempotent insert
milestones.description: `render_toc(headings) -> str` + `update_or_insert(text) -> str` that finds an existing `<!-- TOC -->` ... `<!-- /TOC -->` block and replaces it (or inserts after the first H1 if absent).

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `markdown-toc README.md` twice produces an identical file the second time
CLI exits 1 if there's no H1 AND no TOC marker (told you so)

milestones.technical_note: Idempotency is the load-bearing property — CI hooks run this on every commit.

milestones.title: [CONSOLIDATION] tidy + verify pass
milestones.description: No new public API. Tighten the slugify fixture, add a property test that `slugify(s, seen)` is deterministic given identical input + seen.

acceptance_criteria.description: make verify passes on a clean checkout

milestones.technical_note: [CONSOLIDATION] — REJECT any new feature additions in this milestone.
